source_document.document_identity_notes: Regulator-source PDF from Wisconsin DFI via automated Playwright download. Cover page confirms Molly Maid SPV LLC 2025 FDD.

brand.parent_company: Neighborly Assetco LLC / Dwyer Franchising LLC d/b/a Neighborly / KKR

initial_franchise_fee.notes: Two-part structure: fixed Initial Franchise Fee of $14,900 plus Territory Fee of $1.00 per Target Household (TH). Territory Fee typically $45,000-$70,000 (standard market) or $30,000-$45,000 (mid-size). Average paid in 2024: ~$55,967; range paid: $37,838-$63,635. All due at signing, nonrefundable. Franchise fee waived for expansion of existing compliant franchisees. Discounts available: VetFran (20% off territory fee), Roll-In (5-50% based on existing business gross sales), Multi-Unit (5-20% based on tenure + optional 5% cash discount), Additional Concept (10% off franchise fee). Software enrollment fee of $1,500 also due at signing (paid to ZorWare).

provenance.exact_text: An initial franchise fee of $14,900... a territory fee equal to $1.00 per Target Household... Territory Fees typically range from $45,000 to $70,000 for a standard-size market and $30,000 to $45,000 for a mid-size market.

royalty_rate.notes: Called 'License Fee.' Progressive tiered structure: 6.5% on first $500K of Gross Sales per calendar year, declining to 3% above $2.8M. Tiers are marginal (like income tax brackets). Year 1 rates for Roll-In franchisees may differ (3% for small/medium, 2.5% for large). Minimum License Fees also apply starting Year 2, based on TH in territory. Some legacy franchisees may maintain preexisting rates on renewal.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, License Fee row and Fees Chart

advertising_and_marketing.structure_description: Multi-component: MAP Contribution 2% of Gross Sales (brand fund) + Local Marketing Requirement ($1/TH/year declining to $0.15/TH as sales increase) + optional Local Marketing Groups (up to 3% of Gross Sales). Lower total mandatory marketing cost than Mosquito Joe but still meaningful.

components.name: MAP Contribution (Brand Fund)
components.notes: Subject to ramp-up for legacy franchisees (pre-Nov 2022 agreements) through 2025. Full 2% applies to all from 2026.

components.name: Local Marketing Requirement
components.notes: $1.00/TH/year at $0-$15K weekly Gross Sales; declines to $0.50 ($15K-$20K), $0.25 ($20K-$25K), $0.15 ($25K+). Franchisor may collect and spend on franchisee's behalf if not met.

components.name: Local Marketing Groups (LMGs)
components.notes: Up to 3% of Gross Sales if franchisor designates an LMG for your market. Counts toward Local Marketing Requirement.

advertising_and_marketing.notes: Marketing structure is less burdensome than Mosquito Joe (no mandatory $37K Direct Marketing Program). MAP is straightforward 2%. Local marketing requirement scales with territory size and declines as sales grow.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, MAP Contribution row, Local Marketing Requirement row, Notes 4 and 5

technology_fee.notes: Two components: (1) Technology Package $58/month to ZorWare (Qvinci, Broadly NPS, FranConnect, 1 Office365 E3, 1 Office365 Exchange); (2) CLEO business management software $405/month to ServiceTitan. Total ~$463/month. Optional QuickBooks via ZorWare $30-$200/month. Additional email accounts: $10.50-$24/month each. May increase up to 30% annually. $1,500 enrollment/training fee at signing.

provenance.item_reference: Item 6, Software System Fees row and Note 6

transfer_fee.formula: $15,000 for single transfer; $15,000 + $2,500 per additional franchise if simultaneous transfer of multiple agreements. If assignee has $5M+ in outside investments, fee is greater of stated amount or franchisor's actual costs. Transferee must also buy transfer initial package (~$4,000).
transfer_fee.notes: May be discounted or waived for transfers to controlled entities or immediate family.

other_fees.notes: Up to 3% of total Gross Sales related to Key Account work, Business Development leads, or call center dispatched work.
Up to $1,000. $2,000 penalty for non-attendance. Does not include travel/lodging.
Payable only in event of transfer where transferee attends training prior to closing.
Up to $100/hour per attendee. May increase up to 30% annually.
First: 50% of cumulative revenue from wrongfully serviced customer. Subsequent: 100%. Second violation: right to terminate.
For modifications to franchise agreement at franchisee's request.
Plus cost of rescheduled audit if due to lack of cooperation.
If software fees unpaid within 30 days of invoice.
From due date until all sums paid.
On unpaid balances. May be collected weekly or monthly via ACH.

line_items.notes: Waived for expansion of existing compliant franchisees.
$1.00 per Target Household. Typical range for standard market. Mid-size market: $30,000-$45,000.

line_items.category: Auto Lease Deposit and Lease Expense
line_items.notes: Minimum 2 vehicles required (1 for estimates, 1 for team). New vehicle purchase: $23,500-$26,500.

line_items.category: Real Estate, Utility Deposits and Three Months Rent

line_items.notes: Includes washer/dryer, desks, chairs, file cabinet, telephone system.

line_items.category: Insurance Deposit and Three Months Insurance Expense
Training Expenses for Travel, Food and Lodging

line_items.notes: Payroll, marketing, gasoline, auto maintenance, internet, general business. Does not include owner salary/draw.

tables.description: 2024 Average and Median Gross Sales Per Cleaning
tables.population: 197 Operators representing 417 franchised businesses (of 448 total). Excludes: 9 opened during period, 13 mid-transfer, 2 unreliable data, 6 territory changes, 25 closed during period.
tables.notes: System-wide average gross sales per cleaning: $173.43. Median: $169.75. Tight distribution between 2nd and 3rd quartiles. Bottom 10% still averages $112/cleaning.

tables.description: 2024 Average and Median Gross Sales Per Target Household
tables.notes: Wide range in Gross Sales/TH ($2.59 to $88.13) suggests significant territory utilization variance. Top 10% generates ~5x more revenue per TH than the system average.
91% recurring customer rate is a very strong signal for business predictability. This is one of the highest recurring rates in any franchise vertical.

tables.description: 2024 Same Business Sales Performance (YoY Growth 2023 vs 2024)
tables.population: 197 Reporting Franchisees operating same 417 businesses in both years.

growth_distribution.bracket: More than 10% and up to 20% growth
More than 5% and up to 10% growth
More than 0% and up to 5% growth
More than 0% and up to 5% decline
More than 5% and up to 10% decline
More than 10% and up to 20% decline

tables.notes: 79% of franchisees grew YoY. Only 21% declined. Only 4% declined more than 10%. Healthy same-store-sales picture.

item_19_financial_performance.notes: Item 19 is disclosed with four parts. Unusually rich for cleaning vertical. Provides per-cleaning revenue, per-TH penetration, recurring customer rates, and YoY growth distribution. Does not disclose absolute total gross sales per franchisee (only per-cleaning and per-TH metrics). 25 businesses that closed during 2024 are excluded. No company-owned P&L.

years_reported.notes: All franchised, no company-owned. Net contraction of 20 units. Ceased operations (16) is the largest exit category. California lost 6 units (76→70).
Net contraction of 17 units. Terminations dropped to 2 but ceased operations rose to 22. Illinois: 32→28 (4 ceased). Virginia: 22→19 (3 ceased).

Net contraction of 16 units. System continues to shrink but at a slower pace. Illinois: 28→25 (3 ceased). Kentucky: 7→4 (3 terminations). Puerto Rico: 6→2 (4 ceased). Maryland: 16→14 (2 non-renewals).

action_types.notes: State of Kansas vs. Molly Maid, Inc. (2010). Consent judgment for alleged KCPA violation (franchisee didn't document background checks on employees, gift certificate sales after termination). $25,000 civil penalty + $25,175 costs. Satisfied April 2011. Predecessor entity, not current franchisor.
California v. FOR Franchising LLC d/b/a Window Genie (2017). Affiliate entity, not Molly Maid. Failed to submit ads to CA Commissioner. $5,000 administrative penalty. Concluded.

item_3_litigation.notes: Both actions are concluded and involve predecessor or affiliate entities, not the current Molly Maid SPV LLC franchisor directly. Light litigation profile.

provenance.exact_text: The System was initially developed and offered in Canada in 1979 by Molly Maid International, Inc... Predecessor was originally incorporated as a Michigan corporation on May 8, 1984

warnings: SYSTEM CONTRACTION: Molly Maid has shrunk every year for 3 consecutive years (501→481→464→448). Net loss of 53 units over 3 years (-10.6%).
Royalty is tiered/marginal (6.5% to 3%) — recorded as ambiguous because it cannot be expressed as a single rate.
Technology costs are split: $58/month to ZorWare + $405/month CLEO to ServiceTitan = ~$463/month total. Recorded as ambiguous because of multi-payee structure.
Item 19 does not disclose absolute total gross sales per franchisee — only per-cleaning and per-TH metrics. Cross-brand revenue comparison requires additional calculation.
Initial Fees are two-part (fixed $14,900 franchise fee + variable territory fee), making direct comparison with flat-fee franchises complex.
Entity structure: SPV within Neighborly/KKR securitization chain. All franchise agreements transferred from predecessor to SPV in March 2021.
25 businesses that closed during 2024 are excluded from Item 19 data — survivorship bias applies.

'Ceased operations — other reasons' column in Item 20 includes transfers to existing franchisees, territory consolidations, and abandonments. This inflates the apparent exit rate relative to franchises that simply failed.

null_fields.reason: Two-part fee (franchise fee + territory fee). Cannot express as single value.
Tiered marginal rate from 6.5% to 3%. Cannot express as single rate.
Multi-component: MAP 2% + Local Marketing per TH + optional LMG. No single rate.
Split across two payees ($58/mo ZorWare + $405/mo ServiceTitan). Total ~$463/month.
Both royalty and marketing are ambiguous multi-component structures.
